human_verification.notes: Reviewed package against issue #1408, healthcare domain research contract, records-documentation-and-administration ability contract, and validated specification-based acceptance criteria.

source_classes_covered: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

review_notes: All required healthcare domain research scenario families are mapped to explicit scenario IDs.
All shared, records-documentation-and-administration, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are validated and covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package.
No unreviewed regressions are present in this package build.

covered_required_scenario_families: Unauthorized or unsupported record change request
Coverage, authorization, referral, or documentation prerequisite handling
Safe escalation when patient safety, clinical judgment, or licensed review is required
Refusal when diagnosis, treatment planning, or licensed clinical judgment is requested
Escalation when PHI handling, clinical risk, or payer policy interpretation changes the answer
Normal in-scope execution using realistic healthcare terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when adjacent specialist ownership is required
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary decisions separate healthcare program administration from clinical judgment, payer interpretation, medical necessity, privacy disclosure decisions, compliance signoff, legal interpretation, financial approval, and program policy approval.

Research sources cover public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, recognized healthcare workflow and measure guidance, PHI controls, reporting evidence, and healthcare data terminology.

Scenario map covers missing submission evidence, unsupported record changes, roster conflicts, authorization/referral prerequisites, PHI handling, clinical escalation, low-confidence routing, source conflict, tenant adaptation, and normal administrative execution.
Commercialization and deployment artifacts define specification-based operation and separate retained-implementation promotion criteria.

residual_risks.risk: Tenant-specific payer, plan, program, and jurisdiction rules may change roster, reporting, authorization, privacy, or retention handling.
residual_risks.mitigation: Require tenant facts, source hierarchy, and owner escalation before autonomous action.

residual_risks.risk: PHI-bearing rosters and reporting packets can be over-shared if privacy controls are absent.
residual_risks.mitigation: Escalate disclosure decisions and require minimum-necessary and role-based access handling.

residual_risks.risk: Administrative readiness could be mistaken for clinical, payer, compliance, or program-policy approval.
residual_risks.mitigation: Boundary and refusal scenarios explicitly reject regulated judgment and approval authority.
